name: Mathew Otieno Magundho
title: Computer Security & Digital Forensics Student | SOC / Technical Writer | CTFZone Campus Ambassador
summary: Driven JOOUST undergraduate pairing campus advocacy, technical writing, and community building with hands-on SOC work and capture-the-flag competitions. I blend TryHackMe/CTFZone practice with policy leadership, awareness campaigns, and mentorship to deliver secure, people-centered solutions.
objective: Seeking cybersecurity attachment roles that leverage my SOC, offensive security, and leadership experience across campus organisations and competitive CTF ecosystems.
highlights: BSc Computer Security & Digital Forensics, JOOUST (2023–2027).
Congressman for the School of Informatics & Innovative Systems representing student welfare, governance, and policy.
Campus Ambassador for CTFZone Labs, mentoring peers through realistic labs and SOC-style challenges.
Technical writer for JOOUST ISACA Student Group — translating security insights into awareness campaigns and publications.
Hands-on SOC and offensive practice through TryHackMe labs (e.g., MAC Flooding, Attacktive Directory, SIEM triage) and competitive CTFs (Cyber Chief Circle, Huntress, Cyber Drill).
Experience supporting IT infrastructure rollouts, user onboarding, and knowledge-sharing sessions for Systems Hub Ltd.

cybersecurity: SOC triage & log analysis (Splunk, Wazuh, Arkime)
tools_programming: Network tooling: Nmap, packet analysis, Cisco Packet Tracer

education.program: Bachelor of Science - Computer Security & Digital Forensics
education.details: Relevant coursework: Network Security, Digital Forensics, Data Privacy & Protection, Computer Networks, Operating Systems, Database Systems, and Information Systems Audit.

details: Represent the CTFZone Labs platform across JOOUST, hosting mentor hours and challenge walkthroughs for TryHackMe-style labs.
Boost campus participation in capture-the-flag events by coordinating study groups, content sharings, and LinkedIn reflections.
Collaborate with CTFZone on event promotions, ensuring students access mentorship, labs, and competitive opportunities.

experience.role: Congressman, School of Informatics & Innovative Systems (SIIS)
details: Advocate for student welfare and ICT initiatives in policy, budgeting, and oversight discussions.
Chair congressional committees focused on accountability, communication, and campus cyber awareness.
Serve as the primary liaison between SIIS students, faculty, and the administration.
Draft technical articles and awareness material on topics such as zero-trust, SIEM workflows, and cloud risk management.
Capture event insights from Cyber Chief Circle, Cybersecurity Awareness Week, and ISACA meetups for broader campus communication.
Assisted with LAN deployments, workstation imaging, and network cabling for client sites.
Streamlined ticket processes using Microsoft Office automation and supported user access recovery tasks.
